title: papaver lapponicum subsp. lapponicum
intro: Papaver lapponicum subsp. lapponicum, also known as Arctic poppy or Finnish poppy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Papaver lapponicum subsp. lapponicum include its potential to treat digestive issues, sore throats, and respiratory problems, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve drying the flowers and leaves, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or infuse in oils for topical application.
